title: Bypassing Broken Docker Compose with docker run
rules: When docker-compose fails with KeyError: 'ContainerConfig', remove the container and recreate it with docker run.
Use docker rm -f <container> to forcefully remove the broken container.
Connect the new container to the same network with --network <network_name>.
Pass environment variables explicitly with -e KEY=VALUE instead of relying on docker-compose.
Use docker ps and docker logs to diagnose startup issues.
Old docker-compose v1.29.2 has bugs with newer Docker images. Consider using docker compose (v2) or docker run.
rationale: Old docker-compose versions can fail on container recreation. Direct docker run gives more control.
examples: docker rm -f skv_api && docker run -d --name skv_api --network infrastructure_default ...

anketa_context.session_summary: Fixed Docker deployment issues during SKV refactoring.
